jokes.text: Yo! A swap meet.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil and guest discussing the artistic merit of a sophisticated art gallery
jokes.punchline: Will dismisses the entire upscale art gallery as a swap meet
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's street perspective clashing with high culture. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: This is Mr. Culture.
jokes.setup: Will just called an art gallery a swap meet
jokes.punchline: Phil sarcastically calls Will 'Mr. Culture'
jokes.explanation: Solid setup for Will's art interpretation sequence. Phil's dry delivery contrasts with Will's enthusiasm.

jokes.text: What a brother got to do to get some fries?
jokes.setup: Will claims he can sense what an artist is saying in a painting
jokes.punchline: His interpretation is completely mundane and food-focused
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - takes highbrow art interpretation and makes it about fast food. Very Will-specific voice.

jokes.text: See, and here, I can sense the juxtaposition of metaphors...
jokes.setup: Will using sophisticated art terminology
jokes.punchline: Building to another ridiculous interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good setup using pretentious art speak to build anticipation for the payoff.

jokes.text: ...interspersed with the three-second violation.
jokes.setup: Sophisticated art terminology about juxtaposition of metaphors
jokes.punchline: Basketball reference - three-second violation
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff mixing highbrow art analysis with street basketball knowledge. Classic Will.

jokes.text: You're a jackass.
jokes.setup: Will's ridiculous art interpretations
jokes.punchline: Phil's blunt, exasperated response
jokes.explanation: Phil's frustrated straight-man response. Economical and fits his character's exasperation with Will.

jokes.text: I call this one Workmen's Compensation.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey dealing with Phil's dangerous shoe buffer
jokes.punchline: Names the experience after the insurance claim he'll need
jokes.explanation: Excellent Geoffrey line - dry, dark humor with perfect economy. References both art gallery setting and dangerous appliance.

jokes.text: Autumn, my favorite time of the year. The leaves are falling and the hotties are pledging.
jokes.setup: Will waxing poetic about autumn
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only interested in sorority pledge season
jokes.explanation: Classic Will - takes romantic poetry and makes it about picking up women. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Baby, if I told you you had a beautiful body, would you hold it against me?
jokes.setup: Will approaching a college woman
jokes.punchline: Uses a classic cheesy pickup line
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad pickup line to show Will's approach. Not original but serves character purpose.

jokes.text: Get it? That's a double entendre.
jokes.setup: Will delivers cheesy pickup line
jokes.punchline: Explains his own joke unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Will's cockiness on display - explaining his own bad joke makes it funnier and more cringe.

jokes.text: Oh, Grey Poupon to you too, girl.
jokes.setup: Woman speaks French to Will
jokes.punchline: Will responds with mustard brand name as if it's French
jokes.explanation: Clever play on Will not speaking French but trying to sound sophisticated. Good cultural reference.

jokes.text: And I treat a woman like a king treats a queen.
jokes.setup: Will trying to impress with his chivalrous treatment of women
jokes.punchline: Uses illogical comparison (king treats queen)
jokes.explanation: Will trying to sound romantic but the logic is flawed. Shows his smooth talk falling apart.

jokes.text: Y'all remembered those, huh? Ladies, say goodbye to Will in his native tongue. [ALL BARKING]
jokes.setup: Girls recognize Will's recycled pickup lines
jokes.punchline: They call him a dog and bark at him in unison
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation and public humiliation. The coordinated barking is a memorable visual gag.

jokes.text: [Will barks back]
jokes.setup: Sorority girls bark at Will to humiliate him
jokes.punchline: Will barks back defiantly
jokes.explanation: Will refusing to be beaten down. Physical comedy that shows his resilience and defiance.

jokes.text: Your problem is that you can't understand or respect women... because you're not in touch with your feminine side. / You know what your problem is, C? You're not in touch with anybody's feminine side.
jokes.setup: Carlton lecturing Will about being in touch with his feminine side
jokes.punchline: Will flips it to mock Carlton's lack of success with women
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will comeback - takes Carlton's psychological insight and turns it into a burn about his romantic failures.

jokes.text: Well, what do you think? / It's... It's a... Blue.
jokes.setup: Ashley proudly presents a gift to Phil
jokes.punchline: Phil struggles to find anything positive to say beyond the color
jokes.explanation: Phil's diplomatic struggle with an ugly gift. The hesitation and minimal description show his distress.

jokes.text: Tell me, sir, do I wash that or take a weed whacker to it?
jokes.setup: Phil wearing the hideous sweater Ashley gave him
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey suggests it's so bad it needs yard equipment to clean
jokes.explanation: Another excellent Geoffrey line. Perfectly captures how awful the sweater must look with gardening equipment reference.

jokes.text: Carlton, quick, get a bat, let's bust this piñata.
jokes.setup: Phil wearing the colorful, presumably hideous sweater
jokes.punchline: Will compares it to a piñata that should be destroyed
jokes.explanation: Will's irreverent take on Phil's fashion disaster. Piñata comparison suggests bright, ridiculous colors.

jokes.text: I'd slip on the old dashiki... puff up the old fro... find a foxy mama, say, 'Hey, baby, let's get it on.'
jokes.setup: Phil giving Will advice about confidence with women
jokes.punchline: Phil's dated 70s pickup routine with period-appropriate clothing and language
jokes.explanation: Phil's embarrassing trip down memory lane. Shows generational gap and his outdated approach.

jokes.text: And they bought that? / Actually, your mother was the only one.
jokes.setup: Will incredulous about Phil's pickup success
jokes.punchline: Phil admits his wife was his only success
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment from Phil. Undercuts his confident advice with honest admission of limited success.

jokes.text: [WILL SCREAMING] Get out! What you doing, Barney?
jokes.setup: Will at movies with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Will screams at movie screen, calls character Barney
jokes.explanation: Will's animated movie-watching style. Shows his engaged, vocal personality even in quiet settings.

jokes.text: Yo, man, where Helen at? / She's probably at the chiropractor.
jokes.setup: Will asking where the movie character Helen is
jokes.punchline: Lisa suggests she needs medical attention (implying horror movie violence)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about horror movie violence. Lisa shows she can match Will's commentary style.

jokes.text: Well, if you like this one, wait till you see the sequel. / You are a dog. [WILL BARKS]
jokes.setup: Will making suggestive comment about movie sequel
jokes.punchline: Lisa calls him a dog, Will barks again
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier dog theme. Will's barking is becoming a running gag for this episode.

jokes.text: Little company? I'm more like the Fortune 500, you know? AT&T. Reach out and touch a brother if you got your bill paid.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions wanting 'little company' at the cabin
jokes.punchline: Will inflates himself to corporate level and riffs on AT&T slogan
jokes.explanation: Clever corporate metaphor mixed with 90s advertising reference. Very Will-style wordplay and confidence.

jokes.text: I was so lucky I found this hat. It was the last extra extra extra large.
jokes.setup: Phil wearing matching hat with the hideous sweater
jokes.punchline: Reveals the hat is comically oversized (extra extra extra large)
jokes.explanation: Phil's continued fashion disaster. The triple 'extra' emphasizes the ridiculous size.

jokes.text: And I'd guess there's some laughing and pointing going on too.
jokes.setup: Phil suggesting the gods are smiling on him for finding the hat
jokes.punchline: Vivian suggests the gods are actually mocking him
jokes.explanation: Vivian's dry wit puncturing Phil's delusion about his fashion choices. Good marital dynamic.

jokes.text: Look at this. You could at least yell four!
jokes.setup: Golf ball hits Phil on his property
jokes.punchline: Phil complains about golf etiquette while being assaulted by balls
jokes.explanation: Phil maintaining politeness even while under siege. Shows his proper character even in chaos.

jokes.text: Sir, perhaps you could get them to erect... a fence. / Geoffrey.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey suggesting Phil get protection from golf balls
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause on 'erect,' Phil's uncomfortable response
jokes.explanation: Mild double entendre with Geoffrey's formal language. Phil's reaction suggests awareness of innuendo.

jokes.text: Oh, well, you see, um... I'm not really that good at thinking on my feet.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks Will to perform the love song he just promised
jokes.punchline: Will admits he's bad at improvisation (ironic for his character)
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Will's smooth talk fails when put to the test. Ironic since he's usually quick-witted.

jokes.text: Just call me public enemy number one. / Beat the hell out of playing Stratego with Carlton.
jokes.setup: Will enthusiastically playing along with cops and robbers
jokes.punchline: Compares it favorably to boring board games with Carlton
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will line - references pop culture (Public Enemy) and mocks Carlton in one breath. Good contrast setup.

jokes.text: Oh, I mean, I know my rights. I think I'm entitled to one frisk.
jokes.setup: Will tied up, complaining the ropes are tight
jokes.punchline: Tries to turn bondage into sexual opportunity with legal language
jokes.explanation: Will trying to maintain sexual confidence even while restrained. Mixes legal and sexual innuendo.

jokes.text: Baby, you're kind of killing the mood.
jokes.setup: Lisa revealing her vengeful agenda and restraining Will
jokes.punchline: Will's massive understatement about the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to mortal danger. Classic Will - maintaining cool even in crisis.

jokes.text: Oh, stop bragging, you sick pervert. / Who's a pervert?
jokes.setup: Carlton thinking Will is boasting about kinky activities
jokes.punchline: Phil overhears and wants to know about perverts
jokes.explanation: Good misunderstanding comedy. Phil's innocent curiosity about perverts is awkward.

jokes.text: They're going to terrorize and torture him. / Oh, don't worry, it's all in good fun.
jokes.setup: Carlton describing Will's situation
jokes.punchline: Carlton dismisses torture as harmless fun
jokes.explanation: Carlton's cavalier attitude toward Will's danger. Perfect character moment showing their relationship.

jokes.text: So you mean to tell me for 5 extra dollars, I could get HBO? Okay, basic cable is cool.
jokes.setup: Lisa on phone, presumably with cable company while Will is tied up
jokes.punchline: Will thinks she's ordering cable TV during his captivity
jokes.explanation: Absurd misdirection - Will's mundane assumption about sinister phone call. Good tension relief.

jokes.text: Do I look like a fool to you? / No, not a fool.
jokes.setup: Lisa calling out Will's obvious manipulation
jokes.punchline: Will's diplomatic response avoiding calling her a fool
jokes.explanation: Will's careful word choice while tied up by an unstable person. Shows his survival instincts.

jokes.text: I can't believe you two. Mustard is not the answer. / No, she's right. Get the soy sauce.
jokes.setup: Phil and Vivian trying to clean golf ball stains from their sweaters
jokes.punchline: They're using condiments as cleaning products, escalating to soy sauce
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of bad cleaning choices. The serious discussion about condiments is funny.

jokes.text: Hey, nice sweaters. / Oh, Marachi. Our daughter bought them for us. / She's got great taste.
jokes.setup: Famous basketball player commenting on Phil's hideous sweater
jokes.punchline: Kareem diplomatically calling the awful fashion 'great taste'
jokes.explanation: Celebrity cameo adding to the sweater running gag. Kareem's politeness contrasts with the visual reality.

jokes.text: These are my role models.
jokes.setup: Phil and Vivian looking ridiculous in their matching outfits
jokes.punchline: Ashley earnestly claims them as role models despite their appearance
jokes.explanation: Ashley's innocent perspective on her parents' fashion disaster. Sweet character moment.

jokes.text: How you doing there, Little Miss Mood Swing?
jokes.setup: Lisa's erratic behavior switching from friendly to threatening
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic nickname for her unstable personality
jokes.explanation: Will's sarcastic coping mechanism. Good nickname that captures Lisa's instability.

jokes.text: You need to be worried about getting out. Them rubber rooms ain't got no windows.
jokes.setup: Lisa worried about getting into the sorority
jokes.punchline: Will suggests she should worry about getting into a mental institution
jokes.explanation: Will's street-smart insult about mental health facilities. Dark but fits his survival mode.

jokes.text: In what, bladder control?
jokes.setup: Lisa says the prank was to teach Will a lesson about respecting women
jokes.punchline: Will suggests he learned about bladder control instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will response - deflects serious lesson with crude humor about being scared. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Ugh! I cannot believe you sorority girls could be so devious. / It was actually your cousin Carlton's idea.
jokes.setup: Will blaming sorority girls for the elaborate prank
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals Carlton masterminded it
jokes.explanation: Great plot twist revealing Carlton's involvement. Escalates their rivalry perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? You know what, I'm gonna pop that little zit when I get home.
jokes.setup: Will learning Carlton orchestrated his humiliation
jokes.punchline: Will's threat to 'pop' Carlton like a pimple
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor for Carlton's size and Will's anger. 'Pop that little zit' is vivid and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You don't care. / You're damn right I don't care. You damn near fricasseed me.
jokes.setup: Lisa crying, Will being unsympathetic
jokes.punchline: Will uses cooking term 'fricasseed' for being tortured
jokes.explanation: Excellent word choice - 'fricasseed' elevates the torture into culinary terms. Very Will vocabulary.

jokes.text: I deserve that.
jokes.setup: Lisa presumably hits or attacks Will after his unsympathetic response
jokes.punchline: Will immediately acknowledges he had it coming
jokes.explanation: Will's quick acceptance of consequences. Shows character growth and realistic reaction to retaliation.

jokes.text: I'm a dog. I'm the fresh prince of the pound. The doggiest of all dogs.
jokes.setup: Will making exaggerated apology to help Lisa join sorority
jokes.punchline: Escalates dog metaphors, plays on show title
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that incorporates the show's title. 'Fresh Prince of the Pound' is clever wordplay and perfect escalation.

jokes.text: Hey, wait, wait, wait. Whoa, hold up. What, y'all just gonna leave me hanging up here like a piece of Rotisserie Gold, huh?
jokes.setup: Sorority girls leave after getting what they wanted
jokes.punchline: Will compares himself to rotisserie chicken, still tied up
jokes.explanation: Great visual metaphor - Will tied up like food on a spit. 'Rotisserie Gold' brand reference adds specificity.

jokes.text: We got up there, the girl went crazy. She tied me to this chair, was trying to torture me. / No. / But I got loose, man. Then she really went off. So I grabbed this rock, man...
jokes.setup: Will telling Carlton about his weekend, building to dramatic climax
jokes.punchline: Implies he might have hurt Lisa with a rock
jokes.explanation: Building dramatic tension for the fake-out. Will's acting creates genuine concern before the reveal.

jokes.text: Gotcha.
jokes.setup: Will's dramatic story about potentially hurting Lisa
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was all a prank on Carlton
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word completely reverses the entire emotional setup. Classic misdirection payoff.
